name: 100 E Sugarland Highway teal commercial building
style: one-story commercial building with a tall stepped Art Deco-influenced parapet and rounded central bay

floors.windowPattern: two pairs of tall rectangular windows flanking two central glazed entrances

notes: Turquoise outer bays surround a taller dark-gray rounded central bay.
White projecting trim surrounds all four windows.
Partially visible dark-gray side wall has turquoise vertical pilasters; the service-door position is uncertain.
This corrects the prior unresolved record: repeated views align the teal facade with 886227325, not 886227326.

name: Clewiston Museum & Chamber of Commerce
style: single-story roadside commercial block with a shallow flat parapet and repeated arched facade recesses

floors.windowPattern: openings sit within repeated shallow arched recesses, but parked vehicles prevent a reliable count

notes: Muted light sage-beige facade with a simple parapet cap.
The lower facade is heavily obscured by dealership vehicles.
The geographic target is consistent, but the earlier theatre-versus-museum discrepancy still merits field confirmation.

name: Badcock Home Furniture
style: one-story roadside furniture-store block with a broad mostly blank parapet facade

notes: Broad beige wall with a dark-brown base course.
A nearer building hides the right end, so whole-edge opening counts remain unknown.
Only one daytime frame provides useful evidence; the rest are occluded, distant, or dark.

name: Sugarland Highway dealership office (identity unresolved)
style: small one-story dealership office or showroom block with a flat parapet

notes: Tan commercial facade behind several rows of vehicles.
Lower-wall openings and the wall wordmark are not reliably readable.
This replaces the incompatible Museum descriptor previously assigned to this footprint.
The predicted image region is shifted; identity and wording require better evidence.

name: Mattress Depot multi-tenant commercial block
style: one-story multi-tenant commercial block with varied parapet bays, storefront glazing, and brick accents

floors.windowPattern: multiple full-height storefront-glass bays separated by differently treated tenant sections

notes: Tenant bays use mauve, cream, gray, dark blue, and red-brown brick accents.
Oblique and incomplete views prevent whole-edge opening counts.
Exterior HVAC units and surface-mounted conduit are visible; no windows appear on this edge.
The prior teal descriptor belonged to adjacent 886227325.
One building-level wall color cannot fully represent this multi-tenant facade.
